title: pow: error codes + protocol struct extensions
description:
Add PoW error codes to protocol.rs and extend ChallengeParams/AuthenticateParams.

What: Add 4 new stable error codes and 3 new fields to existing wire structs.

Algorithm:
1. In core/src/protocol.rs, add after RATE_LIMITED:
   pub const POW_REQUIRED: i32 = -32030;  // enrollment requires PoW token
   pub const POW_INVALID: i32 = -32031;   // token failed hash or format check
   pub const POW_STALE: i32 = -32032;     // ts_floor outside ±600s
   pub const POW_REPLAYED: i32 = -32033;  // h16 already seen
2. Add fields to ChallengeParams:
   pub server_salt: String  (base64-encoded 32 random bytes)
   pub difficulty: u8       (leading zero bits required, default 20)
3. Add field to AuthenticateParams:
   pub pow_token: Option<String>  (base64url 31-byte token; None = no PoW required)

Defensive programming:
- Use Option<String> for pow_token (not String) to allow old relays to interop and for test helpers
- Add #[serde(default)] on pow_token so old Authenticate messages without the field still parse
- Error codes are stable wire API — values must never change

Tests: none needed for struct definitions; serialization round-trips tested by existing serde derives
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p nie-core passes; protocol.rs has POW_REQUIRED=-32030, POW_INVALID=-32031, POW_STALE=-32032, POW_REPLAYED=-32033; ChallengeParams has server_salt+difficulty; AuthenticateParams has pow_token: Option<String>
close_reason: protocol.rs: added POW_REQUIRED/INVALID/STALE/REPLAYED error codes; extended ChallengeParams with server_salt+difficulty; extended AuthenticateParams with pow_token: Option<String>
